name: Lateral Movement Suspicion
description: Investigate and contain suspected lateral movement — attacker pivoting from initial foothold to additional hosts using credential abuse, SMB, WMI, or RDP.

steps.name: Map the movement graph — source to destination hosts
steps.description: Identify the full chain: original foothold → intermediate hops → target systems. Document timestamps, protocols used, and credentials leveraged.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would query SIEM for SMB/RDP/WMI connections originating from the suspected foothold host in the attack window.

steps.name: Isolate the source (foothold) host
steps.description: Network-contain the original compromised host to stop further outbound movement. Maintain EDR connectivity for investigation.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would trigger EDR isolation on foothold host. All inbound/outbound blocked except EDR agent.

steps.name: Block attacker-controlled credentials at domain level
steps.description: If specific AD accounts were used for lateral movement, disable them at the domain controller. Do not delete — preserve for investigation.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would run Disable-ADAccount on identified compromised accounts. Accounts disabled not deleted.

steps.name: Force Kerberos ticket purge (klist purge / krbtgt reset if needed)
steps.description: If pass-the-ticket or golden ticket attack is suspected, purge Kerberos tickets on affected hosts and consider krbtgt password reset.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would run klist purge on affected hosts. krbtgt reset requires two sequential resets 10h apart — schedule carefully.

rollback.note: Tickets cannot be restored. Users re-authenticate normally.

steps.name: Scan all reached hosts for persistence mechanisms
steps.description: On each host the attacker reached, scan for: scheduled tasks, services, registry run keys, WMI subscriptions, and startup folder items.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would run EDR live response persistence hunt on all identified destination hosts.

evidence_checklist: Protocol and credentials used for each hop
Persistence artifacts found on destination hosts
Kerberos ticket purge / krbtgt reset confirmation
